headlines.title: Nuclear and Natural Gas Are Teaming Up to Power the AI Data Center Boom
headlines.summary: Data centers are placing unprecedented strain on electric grids across the country and across the world as the rapid integration of artificial intelligence into virtually every market sector unleashes an energy monster that we are woefully unprepared to feed. headlines.title: US Crude Oil, Gasoline Inventories Keep Sinking, but Prices Don’t Care
headlines.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 9.119 million barrels in the week ending June 5. Analysts had expected a 3.4 million draw. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 6.75 million barrels. Despite in

headlines.title: BLM Opens Review of 126,744 Acres for Colorado Oil and Gas Lease Sale
headlines.summary: The Bureau of Land Management has opened a 30-day public scoping period for a proposed December 2026 oil and gas lease sale in Colorado, putting more than 126,000 acres on the table for potential future development. headlines.title: World's Largest Battery Maker Bets Big on Energy Storage
headlines.summary: The world’s top battery manufacturer, CATL, expects sales of battery storage systems to account for half of its total sales in the coming years, signaling strong optimism about the battery storage sector as countries investing in alternative energy seek to make it more reliable.

headlines.title: QatarEnergy Adds Another Oil Discovery to Namibia's Hot Streak
headlines.summary: QatarEnergy has struck oil again offshore Namibia, adding another discovery to what is becoming one of the most impressive exploration runs in the industry. headlines.title: Why Argentina’s Shale Boom Matters for Global Energy Markets
headlines.summary: Argentina’s epic unconventional oil boom is going from strength to strength. At the end of 2024, South America’s third-largest economy emerged as the continent’s fourth-largest oil producer.
